title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the maximum altitude of a projectile, we need to consider the vertical motion. The projectile reaches its maximum height when its vertical velocity is zero.

title: Vertical Motion Equation
text: The vertical motion of the projectile can be described by the equation: $$v_y$$ $$=$$ $$v_0 y$$ - $$g t$$, where $$v_y$$ is the vertical velocity, $$v_0 y$$ is the initial vertical velocity, g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s²), and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Initial Vertical Velocity
text: The initial vertical velocity can be found using the equation: $$v_0 y$$ $$=$$ $$v_0 sin\left(\theta\right)$$, where $$v_0$$ is the initial speed and theta is the launch angle.

title: Time to Reach Maximum Height
text: The time to reach maximum height is when the vertical velocity is zero. Use the equation: $$0$$ $$=$$ $$v_0 y$$ - $$g t_{max}$$, solve for $$t_{max}$$.

title: Calculate Initial Vertical Velocity
text: Given the launch angle is $$30$$ degrees, calculate the initial vertical velocity using $$v_0 y$$ $$=$$ $$v_0 sin\left(30\right)$$.

title: Calculate Time to Maximum Height
text: Using the equation $$0$$ $$=$$ $$v_0 y$$ - $$g t_{max}$$, solve for $$t_{max}$$.

title: Calculate Maximum Altitude
text: Use the equation for vertical displacement: $$h_{max}$$ $$=$$ $$v_0 y t_{max}$$ - $$0.5g {t_{max}}^2$$.

title: Final Calculation
text: Substitute the values to find the maximum altitude.
